sections.paragraph:
-

(1) prop. to strive upward, to mount, to soar, fich emporfchwingen, see Hiph., also the derivatives אֵבֶר and אֶבְרָה. Perhaps this is kindred to עָבַר, עֵבֶר, comp. Pers. ابر eber, ὑπὲρ (super), all of which have the notion of above, being over, and hence of passing over, surpassing, see עָבַר . It is applied-

(2) to any power, and ἐνέργεια, see אָבִיר , אַבִּיר .

Hiphil, to mount upward in flight, as a hawk, Job 39:26. [Derivatives, see Kal.]
